answer: The structure of lục bát has three main rules. First, a couplet has a six-syllable line followed by an eight-syllable line. Second, the 6th syllable of the first line rhymes with the 6th syllable of the second line. Third, the tones of even-numbered syllables follow a strict pattern based on two tonal groups: Flat (ngang, huyền) and Sharp (sắc, hỏi, ngã, nặng). The pattern for the 6-syllable line's even syllables is Flat-Sharp-Flat. The pattern for the 8-syllable line's even syllables is Flat-Sharp-Flat-Flat.
